name: King Pie

ingredients.item: coin (washed and wrapped in aluminum foil)
Shred the orange zest, and set aside. Then, squeeze the entire orange and set aside.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, vanilla powder, cinnamon powder, clove powder, and orange zest. Mix with a large fork.
Whisk the eggs in a smaller bowl. Then add the milk, freshly squeezed orange juice, butter, and brandy. Whisk again.

Pour the liquid mix into the larger bowl. Add the figs, raisins, and walnuts. Mix thoroughly with a large fork (add additional milk, if needed). Toss in the vasilopita coin (a.k.a. “flouri”), making sure it’s well hidden in the batter.

Grease a round 12-inch baking dish with oil or butter. Pour in the batter and place into the oven. Bake for 40minutesutes, or until golden brown. Stick a toothpick or knife in the middle to check if it’s fully baked. If not, bake for another 5 to 10minutesutes.
Remove from the oven, and immediately glaze the top with honey, lathering freely with a pastry brush. Finally, lightly sprinkle sugar (or sift powdered sugar) on top of the honey glaze.
Let the pie cool overnight. Flip the top end over onto a large tea towel. Then flip topside up onto a cake plate or a pizza pan.
